title: Photojournalism
text:
During my time as a reporter and editor for The Daily Northwestern, I had the opportunity to do some sports photojournalism for various Northwestern sports, which was a fun challenge to take on. I also took pictures for stories I wrote for the paper. Check out some of my favorites below.

title: Data Journalism
text:
Inspired by the work of investigative journalists, I completed a data science minor while at Northwestern to give myself the skills necessary to uncover stories I wouldn’t be able to find otherwise.
I took seven courses for the minor and learned skills in statistics, data visualization, data science techniques, data journalism, data cleaning and predictive modeling.
These are some projects I’ve completed for both data science and data journalism courses while I was a student at NU.
Data science class project - download to view!
Data journalism class project - Cook County diversions program data
The Daily Northwestern - campus-wide polling data
Investigative journalism class project - naturalization ceremonies in Chicago amid ICE presence

title: Audio Reporting
text:
I stumbled into audio journalism on accident when one of my friends at The Daily Northwestern asked me to co-byline an audio story with her. Audio reporting opens up opportunities for people to immerse themselves in a story, and it’s a tool I’ve found useful for reaching new audiences.
Prisoncast! - WBEZ Chicago
From September 2025 through December 2025, I traveled to Sheridan Correctional Center in Sheridan, Illinois about once a week to work on a Prisoncast! segment with incarcerated students. The segment, which I co-produced with WBEZ reporter Ari Mejia, aired on WBEZ Chicago on April 12, 2026.
To produce this piece, I worked with two incarcerated students to brainstorm story ideas, come up with interview questions and complete the interview process using a Zoom recorder inside the prison. Outside of the prison, I used Adobe Audition to put the segment together. The opportunity to work on this project strengthened my commitment to uplifting underrepresented voices and leading with curiosity instead of judgment or fear.
More audio stories
A look into The Daily’s coverage of the Deering Meadow encampment
During a period of protest at Northwestern, I worked with top editors at The Daily Northwestern to give people who were not on campus a better idea of what was happening on the ground. This piece won fifth place in the ACP Multimedia Story of the Year podcast division for 2024.
Listen: Gen Z pro-life activists, students convene for annual March for Life









Behind the scenes of my audio story for Medill News Service in Washington, D.C. (Photo credit to fellow reporter Ben Shapiro)

title: Carlotta Angiolillo
text:
Hi! I’m a recent graduate of Northwestern’s Medill School of Journalism and a general assignment reporter at the Columbia Missourian. I’m looking for my next opportunity in journalism once my time with the Missourian ends in August 2026.
I wrote my first article when I was in fifth grade for my elementary school’s student newspaper, and I’ve been hooked on journalism ever since. There’s no doubt in my mind that it’s the best job in the world. The privilege to learn about and share people’s stories is one I treasure, and the responsibility to report the truth and hold power to account is one I take seriously.
I have experience in many different types of journalism, including audio storytelling, data reporting, investigative work, community engagement and legal reporting. I’m always looking for ways to learn new skills and grow as a journalist.
Outside of journalism, I’m usually taking a long walk with a friend, spending too much money at a coffee shop, learning new chords on the guitar or catching up on the latest episode of Abbott Elementary.
You can reach me at lottangiol@gmail.com or 832-728-7576.

title: Print Reporting
text:
I’m a general assignment reporter who can quickly learn new subjects and communicate complex topics to readers. I have bylines in the Houston Chronicle, UPI, The Daily Northwestern and more.
Opponents of median safety ordinance protest rule as ban goes into effect (Columbia Missourian)
